events.description: In the `ArmResourcePropertiesBag` suppression list, the JSONPath `$.definition.SchedulerUpdate...` l

details.body: In the `ArmResourcePropertiesBag` suppression list, the JSONPath `$.definition.SchedulerUpdate...` looks like a typo (missing the trailing `s`). This will prevent the suppression from matching and may cause the rule violation to reappear in CI. Update it to `$.definitions.SchedulerUpdate...` (and consider removing the duplicate `privateLinkResources` entry below if not needed).

## Pull request overview

Adds a new **stable** ARM API version (`2026-02-01`) for the **Microsoft.DurableTask** resource provider, generated from the existing TypeSpec project and wired up in the AutoRest `readme.md`.

**Changes:**
- Updates AutoRest configuration to default to `package-2026-02-01` and adds suppressions for the new tag.
- Introduces the new `2026-02-01` stable Swagger (`durabletask.json`) including new networking/private-link related surface area and `publicNetworkAccess`.
- Ad

events.description: `MyPrivateLinkResource` reads like a placeholder name and it flows into generated OpenAPI artifacts
New api version needs three commits - first should be a direct copy of a stable version, second shou

details.body: New api version needs three commits - first should be a direct copy of a stable version, second should be just the api version change and third should be the actual changes. Could you please add these details on which commits these are?

events.description: Changing this would be a breaking change. We will not do this. The Patch operation dispatches an asy

details.body:
Changing this would be a breaking change. We will not do this. The Patch operation dispatches an async provisioning / configuration step in our data plane.

We prefer merge patch functionality for configuring our policies.

Regardless - this is not a new change and we do not want to introduce a breaking change now. We will keep it in mind for the future.

events.description: It cannot and should not. This is the standard name that must be used. Please see the Private Endpoi

details.body:
It cannot and should not. This is the standard name that must be used. Please see the Private Endpoint integration of other RPs.

It is very obvious that this rule is a false positive, too.

events.description: Yes - this is not anything new. But it seems the old APIs are grandfathered into previous rules some

details.body:
Yes - this is not anything new. But it seems the old APIs are grandfathered into previous rules somehow.

I did not add the suppressions for the old API versions because the automation did not require it.

Surely this is not a blocking comment...

events.description: This is bad advice. Our Patch (merge Patch) operation allows SKU changes which require async provisi

details.body:
This is bad advice. Our Patch (merge Patch) operation allows SKU changes which require async provisioning changes.

Also, changing this operation now would be a breaking change.

events.description: No - this is bad advice. The PATCH operation for Private Endpoint Connections is defined by the Netw

details.body:
No - this is bad advice. The PATCH operation for Private Endpoint Connections is defined by the Network RP team and the RP Platform (RPaaS) managed integration of Private Endpoints. This is not up to us to change.

At the same time, this endpoint must remain async because of the provisioning / configuration work that must be performed upon Patch.

events.description: I have never needed to supply this and nobody has asked for a specific number of commits. Isn't it p

details.body:
> New api version needs three commits - first should be a direct copy of a stable version, second should be just the api version change and third should be the actual changes. Could you please add these details on which commits these are?

I have never needed to supply this and nobody has asked for a specific number of commits. Isn't it pretty clear from the `main.tsp` change? And new folders in `examples`? Everything else is generated from `main.tsp`.

events.description: The docstring says the known values for `api_version` are "2026-02-01" and `None`, but `api_version`

details.body: The docstring says the known values for `api_version` are "2026-02-01" and `None`, but `api_version` is typed as `str` and is used as-is when building requests. Either drop `None` from the documented known values, or make the type `Optional[str]` and explicitly handle `None` (e.g., fall back to the default) to avoid sending an invalid/omitted api-version.

events.description: Same issue as the sync configuration: the docstring lists `None` as a known value for `api_version`,

details.body: Same issue as the sync configuration: the docstring lists `None` as a known value for `api_version`, but the type is `str` and it is passed through into request building. Update the documentation and/or type/validation so `None` is not presented as a supported value unless it’s handled explicitly.

events.description: `serialize_basic(..., data_type="bool")` currently uses `bool(data)`, which will serialize any non-e

details.body: `serialize_basic(..., data_type="bool")` currently uses `bool(data)`, which will serialize any non-empty string (e.g., "false") as `True`. Consider either enforcing that `data` is already a `bool` for this path, or implementing string parsing consistent with `deserialize_basic` (accepting "true"/"false"/"1"/"0").

events.description: The `DurableTaskMgmtClient` docstring lists `None` as a known value for `api_version`, but `api_vers

details.body: The `DurableTaskMgmtClient` docstring lists `None` as a known value for `api_version`, but `api_version` is treated as a string and forwarded into request building. Either remove `None` from the documented known values, or update the typing/handling so `None` is explicitly supported (e.g., fall back to the default).

events.description: The async `DurableTaskMgmtClient` docstring also lists `None` as a known value for `api_version`, bu

details.body: The async `DurableTaskMgmtClient` docstring also lists `None` as a known value for `api_version`, but `api_version` is passed through as a string into request construction. Please align the docs with actual supported values, or make `None` explicitly handled/supported via typing + fallback behavior.

## Pull request overview

Auto-generated refresh of `azure-mgmt-durabletask` from the DurableTask TypeSpec config, updating to API version `2026-02-01` and shipping new networking/private-link surface area as part of a stable `1.1.0` release.

**Changes:**
- Bump package version to `1.1.0` and update metadata/spec commit pointers for `2026-02-01`.
- Add new models/enums and new `SchedulersOperations` APIs for private link resources and private endpoint connections; extend scheduler properties wi

This PR excludes generated test and sample files from the wheel package distribution for the azure-mgmt-durabletask SDK. The change ensures that auto-generated test and sample files are not included in the distributed package, which reduces package size and prevents unnecessary files from being installed.

Key changes:
- Added exclusion patterns for generated test and sample directories

events.description: The import `from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, IO, Iterable, Iterator, List, Optional, TypeVar,

details.body: The import `from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, IO, Iterable, Iterator, List, Optional, TypeVar, Union, cast, overload` has been simplified to remove unused imports like `Dict`, `Iterable`, and `List`. However, `List` is still being used as an alias on line 44. This could cause a runtime error if the `List` alias is used elsewhere in the code.

events.description: [nitpick] Creating an alias `List = list` is unnecessary since the built-in `list` type can be used

details.body:
[nitpick] Creating an alias `List = list` is unnecessary since the built-in `list` type can be used directly. This alias pattern was typically used for backward compatibility but is no longer needed in modern Python typing. Consider removing this alias and using `list` directly in type annotations.
```suggestion

```

events.description: The package is being marked as 'Production/Stable' in the classifier, but this contradicts the previ

details.body:
The package is being marked as 'Production/Stable' in the classifier, but this contradicts the previous beta status (1.0.0b2). Ensure this change aligns with the actual stability and production readiness of the package, as this affects how users perceive the package maturity.
```suggestion
    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
```

## Pull Request Overview

This is an auto-release PR updating the azure-mgmt-durabletask package from version 1.0.0b2 to 1.0.0, transitioning from a preview API version (2025-04-01-preview) to GA version (2025-11-01). The update includes new features and modernizes the package structure with pyproject.toml build configuration.

- Updates API version from 2025-04-01-preview to 2025-11-01
- Adds cloud_setting parameter support and new SchedulerSkuName enum
- Migrates from setup.py to pyproject.tom

events.description: New public type name `OptionalPropertiesUpdateableProperties` uses the uncommon spelling “Updateable

details.body: New public type name `OptionalPropertiesUpdateableProperties` uses the uncommon spelling “Updateable”. Across other ARM Go SDK packages the established convention is “Updatable” (e.g., `SessionPoolUpdatableProperties` in armappcontainers). Consider renaming this model in the TypeSpec/spec (e.g., via a client name override) so the generated Go type becomes `OptionalPropertiesUpdatableProperties` before the stable release is finalized, to avoid locking in an inconsistent public API name.

details.body:
## Pull request overview

Updates the TypeSpec source reference for the `Azure.ResourceManager.DurableTask` SDK by pointing `tsp-location.yaml` at a newer commit in `Azure/azure-rest-api-specs`, ensuring generation pulls the intended latest specification snapshot.

**Changes:**
- Updated the `commit` SHA in `tsp-location.yaml` for DurableTask management spec resolution.

Approved from DurableTask team

events.description: The API listing files in the `api/` directory have not been updated to reflect the new public API su

details.body: The API listing files in the `api/` directory have not been updated to reflect the new public API surface introduced in this PR. All new types (PrivateEndpointConnectionResource, SchedulerPrivateLinkResource, DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnectionData, PublicNetworkAccess, etc.) and updated model factory signatures are missing from the listings. The API listing must be regenerated by running `pwsh eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 Azure.ResourceManager.DurableTask` and including the generated output in t

events.description: In the pagination logic of both `SchedulersGetPrivateLinksCollectionResultOfT.AsPages` and `Schedule

details.body: In the pagination logic of both `SchedulersGetPrivateLinksCollectionResultOfT.AsPages` and `SchedulersGetPrivateEndpointConnectionsCollectionResultOfT.AsPages`, the continuation token passed to `Page<T>.FromValues` is taken from `nextPage` (the *current* page's next link from the *previous* iteration), rather than from `result.NextLink` (the newly parsed next link). This means the continuation token returned in each page is one iteration behind and will point to the wrong (previous) page instead

## Pull request overview

This PR is an auto-generated SDK update for `Azure.ResourceManager.DurableTask`, bumping the API version from `2025-11-01` to `2026-02-01` and promoting the package from `1.1.0-beta.1` to `1.1.0` (stable release). The main new features are private networking support: Private Link Resources and Private Endpoint Connections for Durable Task Schedulers, plus a new `PublicNetworkAccess` property on the Scheduler.

**Changes:**
- New `SchedulerPrivateLinkResource`/`Scheduler

events.description: Same paging continuation token bug as in `SchedulersGetPrivateLinksCollectionResultOfT`: the continu

details.body: Same paging continuation token bug as in `SchedulersGetPrivateLinksCollectionResultOfT`: the continuation token yielded with each page is the `nextPage` URI from the previous iteration rather than from `result.NextLink`. The value should be `result.NextLink?.IsAbsoluteUri == true ? result.NextLink.AbsoluteUri : result.NextLink?.OriginalString` at the point of yielding.

events.description: Same paging continuation token bug: the continuation token yielded with each page uses `nextPage` (t

details.body: Same paging continuation token bug: the continuation token yielded with each page uses `nextPage` (the prior iteration's next link) instead of `result.NextLink`.

events.description: Same paging continuation token bug: the continuation token yielded with each page uses `nextPage` (t

details.body: Same paging continuation token bug: the continuation token yielded with each page uses `nextPage` (the prior iteration's next link) instead of `result.NextLink`.

events.description: The `tsp-location.yaml` file now has `additionalDirectories:` with a blank value followed by an empt

details.body: The `tsp-location.yaml` file now has `additionalDirectories:` with a blank value followed by an empty line (lines 4–5). According to the repository convention for tsp-location.yaml, an empty `additionalDirectories` list should either be omitted or set to `[]`. Having a bare key with no value and a trailing blank line could cause YAML parse errors or unexpected behavior in the generation tooling. If no additional directories are needed, this key should be removed entirely.

@ArthurMa1978 could you check whether this is OK to merge from your perspective?

Thanks!

## Management Plane SDK Review: Azure.ResourceManager.DurableTask 1.1.0

### Versioning :white_check_mark:
- Version: 1.0.0  1.1.0 (minor bump, appropriate for additive changes)
- `ApiCompatVersion`: 1.0.0 present, no breaking changes detected
- CI build passed all ApiCompat checks

### Breaking Changes :white_check_mark:
- All changes are additive (new private endpoint support types + `PublicNetworkAccess` on scheduler properties)
- No existing APIs were removed or modified

### API Naming Issu

events.description: :red_circle: **Naming Issue**: `OptionalPropertiesUpdateableProperties` is a meaningless name that l

details.body:
:red_circle: **Naming Issue**: `OptionalPropertiesUpdateableProperties` is a meaningless name that leaks TypeSpec/ARM template mechanics. A user reading this API cannot tell this type represents the updateable properties of a private endpoint connection.

**Suggested rename**: `DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnectionPatchProperties`

**Fix**: This type is auto-generated from the ARM `UpdateableProperties<>` template and does **not** exist as a named model in the TypeSpec definition, so `@@clientNam

events.description: :yellow_circle: **Naming Issue**: `PublicNetworkAccess` is a very common type name used across many

details.body:
:yellow_circle: **Naming Issue**: `PublicNetworkAccess` is a very common type name used across many Azure services. It should have the `DurableTask` prefix for disambiguation, consistent with other enum/union types in this SDK.

**Suggested rename**: `DurableTaskPublicNetworkAccess`

**Fix in client.tsp**:
```
@@clientName(PublicNetworkAccess, "DurableTaskPublicNetworkAccess", "csharp");
```

:yellow_circle: **Naming Issue**: `PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties` lacks the `DurableTask` service prefix. Other properties types in this SDK are correctly prefixed (e.g., `DurableTaskSchedulerProperties`, `DurableTaskHubProperties`).

**Suggested rename**: `DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnectionProperties`

**Fix**: This type comes from the ARM common type `Azure.ResourceManager.CommonTypes.PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties` and does **not** exist as a named model in the TypeSpec definitio

:yellow_circle: **Naming Issue**: `PrivateEndpointConnectionPatch` lacks the `DurableTask` service prefix. The analogous patch type for schedulers is `DurableTaskSchedulerPatch`.

**Suggested rename**: `DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnectionPatch`

**Fix**: This type is auto-generated from the ARM `PrivateEndpointConnectionUpdate` template in `private-endpoints.tsp` and does **not** exist as a named model in the TypeSpec definition, so `@@clientName` cannot be used. Instead, use **SDK-side custom

:yellow_circle: **Naming Issue**: `PrivateEndpointConnectionResource` and `PrivateEndpointConnectionCollection` lack the `DurableTask` prefix. This is inconsistent with the data type `DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnectionData` which IS correctly prefixed.

**Suggested rename**: `DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnectionResource` / `DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnectionCollection`

**Fix in client.tsp**:
```
@@clientName(PrivateEndpointConnection, "DurableTaskPrivateEndpointConnection", "csharp");
```

## Pull Request Overview

This PR performs final class renaming for the Azure DurableTask SDK version 1.0.0 release. The changes focus on removing the "DurableTask" prefix from model class names to achieve cleaner, more concise naming conventions.

Key changes include:
- Renaming `DurableTaskPurgeableOrchestrationState` to `PurgeableOrchestrationState`
- Renaming `DurableTaskResourceRedundancyState` to `ResourceRedundancyState`
- Updating all references in test files and API surface definitions

## Pull Request Overview

This PR prepares the Azure.ResourceManager.DurableTask SDK for its first stable v1.0.0 release, upgrading from the previous beta version.

- Updates specification commit reference and assets tag
- Adds comprehensive test coverage for task hubs and retention policies
- Improves test reliability by replacing hardcoded values with strongly-typed enums

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 6 out of 29 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>

The assertion message is incorrect. This test is deleting a retention policy, not a scheduler. The message should be 'Retention policy deleted successfully'.
```suggestion
                Assert.Pass("Retention policy deleted successfully");
```

events.description: getApiVersionFromUrl uses `new URL(urlStr)` without a base URL. In @azure/core-lro, `initialRequestU

details.body: getApiVersionFromUrl uses `new URL(urlStr)` without a base URL. In @azure/core-lro, `initialRequestUrl` can be a relative request path (e.g. "path"), which would cause this to throw when restoring a poller. Consider using `new URL(urlStr, "https://microsoft.com")` (or similar) and reading `searchParams` from that.

events.description: The comment for `normalizeUnreserved` is incorrect/incomplete: RFC 3986 unreserved characters are AL

details.body: The comment for `normalizeUnreserved` is incorrect/incomplete: RFC 3986 unreserved characters are ALPHA / DIGIT / "-" / "." / "_" / "~", not just "-" / "." / "~". Either update the comment/function to match RFC 3986, or rename/document this as a targeted normalization (e.g. only decoding `%2D` in parameter names).

events.description: addApiVersionToUrl builds the URL by string-concatenating onto the original `url`. This can produce

details.body: addApiVersionToUrl builds the URL by string-concatenating onto the original `url`. This can produce malformed URLs for valid inputs (e.g. URLs with an empty query string ending in `?`, or URLs with a `#fragment`, where the query must be inserted before the fragment). Consider using the parsed `URL` object to set `searchParams` and then reconstructing the URL (also ensure `apiVersion` is URL-encoded).

events.description: This file reintroduces a second copy of addApiVersionToUrl with the same string-concatenation approa

details.body: This file reintroduces a second copy of addApiVersionToUrl with the same string-concatenation approach as pollingHelpers.ts. Besides the same edge-case URL correctness issues, this duplication makes the helpers likely to drift over time. Consider factoring this into a shared helper used by both pagingHelpers.ts and pollingHelpers.ts.

events.description: The “cross-platform” example under “run a single sample with the required environment variables set”

details.body: The “cross-platform” example under “run a single sample with the required environment variables set” does not actually set any environment variables (it just runs `node ...`). This is inconsistent with the pattern used across the repo (e.g. `npx cross-env VAR=value node ...`) and with the added `cross-env` devDependency. Update the example to demonstrate setting the required env vars via `npx cross-env`, or adjust the surrounding text if no env vars are needed.

events.description: The “cross-platform” example says it runs the sample with required environment variables set, but th

details.body: The “cross-platform” example says it runs the sample with required environment variables set, but the command shown (`node operationsListSample.js`) doesn’t set any env vars. This is inconsistent with the standard samples pattern in this repo (typically `npx cross-env VAR=value node ...`) and with the added `cross-env` devDependency. Update the example to use `npx cross-env` (with the actual env var names) or revise the text.

## Pull request overview

Regenerates `@azure/arm-durabletask` from the DurableTask.Management TypeSpec configuration for API version **2026-02-01** and prepares a **1.1.0** stable release, including new Scheduler private endpoint/private link surface area and updated generated helpers/samples.

**Changes:**
- Bumps package version to **1.1.0** and updates TypeSpec commit reference for generation inputs.
- Adds new Scheduler operations/models for **private endpoint connections** and **private li

events.description: The export targets for the API subpaths use "src/..." instead of "./src/...". Other packages’ warp c

details.body: The export targets for the API subpaths use "src/..." instead of "./src/...". Other packages’ warp configs consistently use "./src/..."; leaving off the leading "./" may break module resolution during build/publish. Update these 4 entries to use "./src/..." for consistency and correctness.

LGTM
cc @JialinHuang803
@JialinHuang803 can you merge :)

events.description: Practically, it's low-risk today — warp uses path.resolve(packageRoot, sourcePath) internally, which

details.body:
Practically, it's low-risk today — warp uses path.resolve(packageRoot, sourcePath) internally, which resolves both "src/..." and "./src/..." identically. The generated dist paths in package.json always get "./" prepended.

But I would suggest that we maintain consistency by always adding the "./" prefix in the emitter. cc.@kazrael2119

## Pull Request Overview

This PR updates the Azure DurableTask ARM SDK from a beta preview version to a stable release, transitioning from API version 2025-04-01-preview to 2025-11-01 stable. The changes reflect the package moving from beta (1.0.0-beta.2) to stable (1.0.0) status.

### Key Changes:
- Updates API version from 2025-04-01-preview to 2025-11-01 stable
- Changes package version from 1.0.0-beta.2 to 1.0.0 
- Adds cloud setting helpers for multi-cloud support
- Improves TypeScript imp

events.description: Using the generic 'Function' type bypasses type safety. Consider defining a more specific function s

details.body: The conditional logic for handling empty values is duplicated in multiple places (lines 76-80, 95-99, 154-158). Consider extracting this into a helper function to reduce code duplication and improve maintainability.
